title: bactris acanthocarpa subsp. trailiana
intro: Bactris acanthocarpa subsp. trailiana, also known as a palm tree species native to the Andes mountains, is a type of plant that belongs to the Arecaceae family. The medicinal properties of Bactris acanthocarpa subsp. trailiana include its ability to be used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Bactris acanthocarpa subsp. trailiana can be prepared as herbal infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine in its raw form.
